1	"""Post-recording orchestrator — trim → transcribe → title → upload.
2	
3	Each stage is a stub in this prompt. Prompts 07-10 replace the bodies:
4	
5	- ``_stage_trim``: prompt 08 (silence trimming)
6	- ``_stage_transcribe``: prompt 07 (full-session transcription)
7	- ``_stage_title``: prompt 09 (auto-title via Claude)
8	- ``_stage_upload``: prompt 10 (Codex upload with credentials)
9	
10	The modal talks to the pipeline only through Qt signals, so replacements
11	drop in without touching UI code.
12	"""
13	
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
17	from pathlib import Path
18	from typing import Optional
19	
20	from PyQt6.QtCore import QObject, QThread, pyqtSignal
21	
22	
23	STAGE_ORDER: tuple[str, ...] = ("trim", "transcribe", "title", "upload")
24	
25	
26	@dataclass
27	class RecordingArtifact:
28	    audio_path: Path
29	    duration_seconds: float
30	    sample_rate: int
31	    channels: int
32	
33	
34	@dataclass
35	class PipelineResult:
36	    trimmed_audio: Optional[Path] = None
37	    trim_intervals: list = field(default_factory=list)
38	    original_duration_s: Optional[float] = None
39	    trimmed_duration_s: Optional[float] = None
40	    transcript: Optional[str] = None
41	    transcript_segments: list = field(default_factory=list)
42	    transcript_language: Optional[str] = None
43	    transcript_model: Optional[str] = None
44	    title: Optional[str] = None
45	    title_method: Optional[str] = None  # "llm" | "fallback"
46	    recorded_date: Optional[str] = None  # ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD), UTC
47	    upload_id: Optional[str] = None
48	    codex_url: Optional[str] = None
49	    errors: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
50	
51	
52	class RecordingPipeline(QObject):
53	    """Runs trim → transcribe → title → upload. Each stage emits a signal
54	    with its result or error."""
55	
56	    stage_started = pyqtSignal(str)
57	    stage_succeeded = pyqtSignal(str, object)
58	    stage_failed = pyqtSignal(str, str)
59	    # (stage_name, percent 0-100). Only the upload stage emits this today.
60	    stage_progress = pyqtSignal(str, int)
61	    all_done = pyqtSignal(object)
62	
63	    def __init__(self, artifact: RecordingArtifact, parent: QObject | None = None):
64	        super().__init__(parent)
65	        self.artifact = artifact
66	        self.result = PipelineResult()
67	
68	    def run(self) -> None:
69	        stages = (
70	            ("trim", self._stage_trim),
71	            ("transcribe", self._stage_transcribe),
72	            ("title", self._stage_title),
73	            ("upload", self._stage_upload),
74	        )
75	        try:
76	            for name, fn in stages:
77	                self.stage_started.emit(name)
78	                try:
79	                    value = fn()
80	                except Exception as exc:
81	                    self.result.errors[name] = str(exc)
82	                    self.stage_failed.emit(name, str(exc))
83	                    return
84	                self.stage_succeeded.emit(name, value)
85	        finally:
86	            self.all_done.emit(self.result)
87	
88	    def _stage_trim(self):
89	        from dialectic.auto_trim import auto_trim
90	
91	        out_path = self.artifact.audio_path.with_suffix(".trimmed.wav")
92	        result = auto_trim(self.artifact.audio_path, out_path)
93	        self.result.trimmed_audio = result.output_path
94	        self.result.trim_intervals = list(result.intervals)
95	        self.result.original_duration_s = result.original_duration_s
96	        self.result.trimmed_duration_s = result.trimmed_duration_s
97	        return result
98	
99	    def _stage_transcribe(self):
100	        from dialectic.batch_transcriber import transcribe
101	
102	        # `trimmed_audio` is populated by `_stage_trim` (prompt 08 replaces
103	        # its body; prompt 06's stub already assigns the raw path). Fall
104	        # back to the raw artifact path so this stage also works in
105	        # isolation — e.g., a test that bypasses trim.
106	        source = self.result.trimmed_audio or self.artifact.audio_path
107	        result = transcribe(source)
108	        self.result.transcript = result.text
109	        self.result.transcript_segments = list(result.segments)
110	        self.result.transcript_language = result.language
111	        self.result.transcript_model = result.model_name
112	        return result
113	
114	    def _stage_title(self):
115	        # Title generation must never fail the whole pipeline. The
116	        # deterministic fallback inside ``generate_title`` is always
117	        # available; the outer try/except exists only for unexpected
118	        # errors above that layer (e.g. an import-time failure).
119	        from dialectic.auto_title import (
120	            AutoTitleResult,
121	            _deterministic_fallback,
122	            generate_title,
123	        )
124	
125	        try:
126	            duration = (
127	                self.result.trimmed_duration_s
128	                or self.artifact.duration_seconds
129	            )
130	            result = generate_title(self.result.transcript or "", duration)
131	        except Exception:
132	            duration = self.artifact.duration_seconds
133	            result = AutoTitleResult(
134	                title=_deterministic_fallback(duration),
135	                recorded_date="",
136	                method="fallback",
137	                warnings=["unexpected error in generate_title"],
138	            )
139	
140	        self.result.title = result.title
141	        self.result.title_method = result.method
142	        self.result.recorded_date = result.recorded_date
143	        return result
144	
145	    def _stage_upload(self):
146	        # Real Codex upload: prepare → PUT → finalize, with a progress
147	        # callback for the UI and a pending-queue fallback so a flaky
148	        # network doesn't lose the recording. The trimmed .wav +
149	        # transcript stay on disk; a later `drain()` replays them.
150	        from datetime import datetime, timezone
151	
152	        from dialectic.codex_upload import UploadError, upload_recording
153	        from dialectic.credentials import active as active_credentials
154	        from dialectic.pending_queue import PendingUpload, enqueue
155	
156	        creds = active_credentials()
157	        if creds is None or not creds.api_key or not creds.codex_url:
158	            # No credentials → don't even try; queue for later so the
159	            # audio isn't lost on the next run of `Retry pending
160	            # uploads`. Return a result dict so callers can inspect
161	            # what happened.
162	            raise RuntimeError(
163	                "no Codex credentials configured — sign in to upload"
164	            )
165	
166	        audio_path = self.result.trimmed_audio or self.artifact.audio_path
167	        transcript = self.result.transcript or ""
168	        title = self.result.title or ""
169	        recorded_date = self.result.recorded_date or ""
170	        extraction_method = (
171	            f"dialectic-{self.result.transcript_model or 'faster-whisper'}"
172	        )
173	
174	        try:
175	            upload_result = upload_recording(
176	                audio_path=audio_path,
177	                transcript=transcript,
178	                title=title,
179	                recorded_date=recorded_date,
180	                codex_url=creds.codex_url,
181	                api_key=creds.api_key,
182	                extraction_method=extraction_method,
183	                on_progress=lambda sent, total: self.stage_progress.emit(
184	                    "upload", int(sent * 100 / max(total, 1))
185	                ),
186	            )
187	        except UploadError as e:
188	            enqueue(
189	                PendingUpload(
190	                    audio_path=str(audio_path),
191	                    transcript=transcript,
192	                    title=title,
193	                    recorded_date=recorded_date,
194	                    extraction_method=extraction_method,
195	                    created_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
196	                )
197	            )
198	            # Re-raise so the outer run() loop's except branch emits
199	            # stage_failed with a message explaining the user can retry
200	            # from the Dialectic menu later.
201	            raise RuntimeError(
202	                f"{e} — queued for retry "
203	                f"(Dialectic menu → 'Retry pending uploads')"
204	            ) from e
205	
206	        self.result.upload_id = upload_result.upload_id
207	        self.result.codex_url = upload_result.codex_url
208	        return upload_result
209	
210	
211	class PipelineThread(QThread):
212	    """Runs a RecordingPipeline off the UI thread so a slow stage can't
213	    freeze the modal."""
214	
215	    def __init__(self, pipeline: RecordingPipeline, parent: QObject | None = None):
216	        super().__init__(parent)
217	        self.pipeline = pipeline
218	
219	    def run(self) -> None:  # noqa: D401 — QThread override
220	        self.pipeline.run()
221	
222	
223	__all__ = [
224	    "STAGE_ORDER",
225	    "RecordingArtifact",
226	    "PipelineResult",
227	    "RecordingPipeline",
228	    "PipelineThread",
229	]

file.content:
"""Post-recording orchestrator — trim → transcribe → title → upload.

Each stage is a stub in this prompt. Prompts 07-10 replace the bodies:

- ``_stage_trim``: prompt 08 (silence trimming)
- ``_stage_transcribe``: prompt 07 (full-session transcription)
- ``_stage_title``: prompt 09 (auto-title via Claude)
- ``_stage_upload``: prompt 10 (Codex upload with credentials)

The modal talks to the pipeline only through Qt signals, so replacements
drop in without touching UI code.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional

from PyQt6.QtCore import QObject, QThread, pyqtSignal


STAGE_ORDER: tuple[str, ...] = ("trim", "transcribe", "title", "upload")


@dataclass
class RecordingArtifact:
    audio_path: Path
    duration_seconds: float
    sample_rate: int
    channels: int


@dataclass
class PipelineResult:
    trimmed_audio: Optional[Path] = None
    trim_intervals: list = field(default_factory=list)
    original_duration_s: Optional[float] = None
    trimmed_duration_s: Optional[float] = None
    transcript: Optional[str] = None
    transcript_segments: list = field(default_factory=list)
    transcript_language: Optional[str] = None
    transcript_model: Optional[str] = None
    title: Optional[str] = None
    title_method: Optional[str] = None  # "llm" | "fallback"
    recorded_date: Optional[str] = None  # ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD), UTC
    upload_id: Optional[str] = None
    codex_url: Optional[str] = None
    errors: dict = field(default_factory=dict)


class RecordingPipeline(QObject):
    """Runs trim → transcribe → title → upload. Each stage emits a signal
    with its result or error."""

    stage_started = pyqtSignal(str)
    stage_succeeded = pyqtSignal(str, object)
    stage_failed = pyqtSignal(str, str)
    # (stage_name, percent 0-100). Only the upload stage emits this today.
    stage_progress = pyqtSignal(str, int)
    all_done = pyqtSignal(object)

    def __init__(self, artifact: RecordingArtifact, parent: QObject | None = None):
        super().__init__(parent)
        self.artifact = artifact
        self.result = PipelineResult()

    def run(self) -> None:
        stages = (
            ("trim", self._stage_trim),
            ("transcribe", self._stage_transcribe),
            ("title", self._stage_title),
            ("upload", self._stage_upload),
        )
        try:
            for name, fn in stages:
                self.stage_started.emit(name)
                try:
                    value = fn()
                except Exception as exc:
                    self.result.errors[name] = str(exc)
                    self.stage_failed.emit(name, str(exc))
                    return
                self.stage_succeeded.emit(name, value)
        finally:
            self.all_done.emit(self.result)

    def _stage_trim(self):
        from dialectic.auto_trim import auto_trim

        out_path = self.artifact.audio_path.with_suffix(".trimmed.wav")
        result = auto_trim(self.artifact.audio_path, out_path)
        self.result.trimmed_audio = result.output_path
        self.result.trim_intervals = list(result.intervals)
        self.result.original_duration_s = result.original_duration_s
        self.result.trimmed_duration_s = result.trimmed_duration_s
        return result

    def _stage_transcribe(self):
        from dialectic.batch_transcriber import transcribe

        # `trimmed_audio` is populated by `_stage_trim` (prompt 08 replaces
        # its body; prompt 06's stub already assigns the raw path). Fall
        # back to the raw artifact path so this stage also works in
        # isolation — e.g., a test that bypasses trim.
        source = self.result.trimmed_audio or self.artifact.audio_path
        result = transcribe(source)
        self.result.transcript = result.text
        self.result.transcript_segments = list(result.segments)
        self.result.transcript_language = result.language
        self.result.transcript_model = result.model_name
        return result

    def _stage_title(self):
        # Title generation must never fail the whole pipeline. The
        # deterministic fallback inside ``generate_title`` is always
        # available; the outer try/except exists only for unexpected
        # errors above that layer (e.g. an import-time failure).
        from dialectic.auto_title import (
            AutoTitleResult,
            _deterministic_fallback,
            generate_title,
        )

        try:
            duration = (
                self.result.trimmed_duration_s
                or self.artifact.duration_seconds
            )
            result = generate_title(self.result.transcript or "", duration)
        except Exception:
            duration = self.artifact.duration_seconds
            result = AutoTitleResult(
                title=_deterministic_fallback(duration),
                recorded_date="",
                method="fallback",
                warnings=["unexpected error in generate_title"],
            )

        self.result.title = result.title
        self.result.title_method = result.method
        self.result.recorded_date = result.recorded_date
        return result

    def _stage_upload(self):
        # Real Codex upload: prepare → PUT → finalize, with a progress
        # callback for the UI and a pending-queue fallback so a flaky
        # network doesn't lose the recording. The trimmed .wav +
        # transcript stay on disk; a later `drain()` replays them.
        from datetime import datetime, timezone

        from dialectic.codex_upload import UploadError, upload_recording
        from dialectic.credentials import active as active_credentials
        from dialectic.pending_queue import PendingUpload, enqueue

        creds = active_credentials()
        if creds is None or not creds.api_key or not creds.codex_url:
            # No credentials → don't even try; queue for later so the
            # audio isn't lost on the next run of `Retry pending
            # uploads`. Return a result dict so callers can inspect
            # what happened.
            raise RuntimeError(
                "no Codex credentials configured — sign in to upload"
            )

        audio_path = self.result.trimmed_audio or self.artifact.audio_path
        transcript = self.result.transcript or ""
        title = self.result.title or ""
        recorded_date = self.result.recorded_date or ""
        extraction_method = (
            f"dialectic-{self.result.transcript_model or 'faster-whisper'}"
        )

        try:
            upload_result = upload_recording(
                audio_path=audio_path,
                transcript=transcript,
                title=title,
                recorded_date=recorded_date,
                codex_url=creds.codex_url,
                api_key=creds.api_key,
                extraction_method=extraction_method,
                on_progress=lambda sent, total: self.stage_progress.emit(
                    "upload", int(sent * 100 / max(total, 1))
                ),
            )
        except UploadError as e:
            enqueue(
                PendingUpload(
                    audio_path=str(audio_path),
                    transcript=transcript,
                    title=title,
                    recorded_date=recorded_date,
                    extraction_method=extraction_method,
                    created_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
                )
            )
            # Re-raise so the outer run() loop's except branch emits
            # stage_failed with a message explaining the user can retry
            # from the Dialectic menu later.
            raise RuntimeError(
                f"{e} — queued for retry "
                f"(Dialectic menu → 'Retry pending uploads')"
            ) from e

        self.result.upload_id = upload_result.upload_id
        self.result.codex_url = upload_result.codex_url
        return upload_result


class PipelineThread(QThread):
    """Runs a RecordingPipeline off the UI thread so a slow stage can't
    freeze the modal."""

    def __init__(self, pipeline: RecordingPipeline, parent: QObject | None = None):
        super().__init__(parent)
        self.pipeline = pipeline

    def run(self) -> None:  # noqa: D401 — QThread override
        self.pipeline.run()


__all__ = [
    "STAGE_ORDER",
    "RecordingArtifact",
    "PipelineResult",
    "RecordingPipeline",
    "PipelineThread",
]

content.content:
1	"""
2	Process Theseus Codex uploads locally.
3	
4	Story
5	-----
6	The Codex web app (on Vercel) accepts file uploads into its Postgres
7	database but cannot run the Noosphere Python pipeline in that runtime —
8	Vercel's serverless Node has no Python interpreter. Uploads land as
9	``queued_offline`` with their ``textContent`` sitting in the DB until
10	someone runs this command on a machine that DOES have Noosphere.
11	
12	What it does
13	------------
14	Given an Upload id, this module:
15	
16	1. Connects to the Codex's Postgres (the shared Supabase instance — same
17	   DB, different tables than Noosphere would normally write to).
18	2. Reads the ``Upload`` row, pulls ``textContent``.
19	3. Extracts atomic claims from the text. Two extraction modes:
20	     - default / naive: rule-based sentence splitter that keeps only
21	       assertoric-looking sentences (filters questions, fragments, mere
22	       listings). Zero dependencies beyond the standard library, works
23	       without an LLM API key.
24	     - ``--with-llm``: delegates to the registered ``extract_claims``
25	       method (the Noosphere LLM pipeline). Higher quality, requires
26	       ``ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`` or ``OPENAI_API_KEY`` via noosphere.config.
27	4. Writes each extracted claim back into the Codex's ``Conclusion``
28	   table (tier=``open``, confidence=0.5) so it appears in the Codex web
29	   UI immediately.
30	5. Updates the Upload row to ``status='ingested'`` and sets
31	   ``claimsCount`` so the dashboard counters reflect the work.
32	6. Writes an ``AuditEvent`` row for traceability.
33	
34	Why write to the Codex schema directly instead of Noosphere's own
35	-----------------------------------------------------------------
36	The goal of this command is "user uploaded a file, user sees something
37	in the Codex UI after running Noosphere locally". Writing to Noosphere's
38	separate tables would require the Codex UI to also query those tables
39	(the existing Python-spawn bridges do this, but they don't work on
40	Vercel either). Writing directly to the Codex's ``Conclusion`` table
41	gives the end-to-end "I uploaded, I can see analysis" flow without any
42	additional infrastructure. Full Noosphere-side artifact/chunk/claim
43	storage can be added later via ``--also-write-noosphere``.
44	"""
45	
46	from __future__ import annotations
47	
48	import json
49	import os
50	import re
51	import sys
52	import uuid
53	from dataclasses import dataclass
54	from datetime import datetime, timezone
55	from typing import Optional
56	
57	try:
58	    import psycopg2
59	    import psycopg2.extras
60	except ImportError as e:  # pragma: no cover
61	    raise RuntimeError(
62	        "psycopg2 is required for the Codex bridge. "
63	        "Install with: pip install psycopg2-binary"
64	    ) from e
65	
66	
67	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
68	# SQLite shim — test-only codex connection.
69	#
70	# Prod always points at Supabase / Postgres. The ingest test suite spins up an
71	# in-process SQLite DB seeded from ``tests/fixtures/minimal_codex_schema.sql``
72	# so the pipeline SQL can be exercised without a running Postgres. The shim
73	# below is the cheapest adapter that lets the (otherwise psycopg2-shaped)
74	# pipeline run unchanged under either backend: translate ``%s`` → ``?``,
75	# yield dict-shaped rows, and expose the handful of cursor/connection methods
76	# the pipeline actually calls.
77	#
78	# Triggered by a ``sqlite://<path>`` URL; anything else goes to psycopg2.
79	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
80	
81	
82	class _SqliteCodexCursor:
83	    """Translate psycopg2-style calls to sqlite3 calls and return dict rows."""
84	
85	    def __init__(self, cur):
86	        self._cur = cur
87	
88	    @staticmethod
89	    def _translate(query: str) -> str:
90	        return query.replace("%s", "?")
91	
92	    def execute(self, query, params=()):
93	        self._cur.execute(self._translate(query), params or ())
94	        return self
95	
96	    def executemany(self, query, seq):
97	        self._cur.executemany(self._translate(query), seq)
98	        return self
99	
100	    def fetchone(self):
101	        row = self._cur.fetchone()
102	        return dict(row) if row is not None else None
103	
104	    def fetchall(self):
105	        return [dict(r) for r in self._cur.fetchall()]
106	
107	    def __iter__(self):
108	        for row in self._cur:
109	            yield dict(row)
110	
111	    @property
112	    def rowcount(self):
113	        return self._cur.rowcount
114	
115	    def close(self):
116	        self._cur.close()
117	
118	
119	class _SqliteCodexConn:
120	    def __init__(self, conn):
121	        self._conn = conn
122	
123	    def cursor(self, *_args, **_kwargs):
124	        return _SqliteCodexCursor(self._conn.cursor())
125	
126	    def commit(self):
127	        self._conn.commit()
128	
129	    def rollback(self):
130	        self._conn.rollback()
131	
132	    def close(self):
133	        self._conn.close()
134	
135	
136	def _open_codex_connection(url: str):
137	    """Open a Codex DB connection. ``sqlite://<path>`` triggers the test
138	    shim; anything else is treated as a Postgres URL for psycopg2."""
139	    if url.startswith("sqlite://"):
140	        import sqlite3
141	
142	        path = url[len("sqlite://"):]
143	        conn = sqlite3.connect(path)
144	        conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
145	        return _SqliteCodexConn(conn)
146	    return psycopg2.connect(url)
147	
148	
149	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
150	# Postgres-safe sanitization.
151	#
152	# Postgres's UTF-8 enforcement rejects strings containing the NUL
153	# byte (``\u0000``) with "invalid byte sequence for encoding "UTF8":
154	# 0x00". PDF extraction, DOCX extraction, and even LLM output
155	# occasionally produce them. Every string that's about to be inserted
156	# into the Codex DB flows through ``_db_safe`` first so one bad
157	# byte doesn't nuke the whole ingest.
158	#
159	# We also strip other C0 control chars (except tab/newline/CR) and
160	# lone UTF-16 surrogate halves, which behave the same way on some
161	# server collations.
162	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
163	
164	# C0 controls minus \t (\x09), \n (\x0a), \r (\x0d). Also nuke BOM.
165	_DB_UNSAFE = re.compile(r"[\x00-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\ufeff]")
166	_DB_SURROGATE = re.compile(r"[\ud800-\udfff]")
167	
168	
169	def _db_safe(s: Optional[str], *, cap: Optional[int] = None) -> str:
170	    """Scrub for Postgres and optionally cap length.
171	
172	    ``None`` / non-string inputs return ``""`` so callers can unconditionally
173	    pass field values without null-checking first.
174	    """
175	    if s is None:
176	        return ""
177	    text = str(s)
178	    text = _DB_UNSAFE.sub("", text)
179	    text = _DB_SURROGATE.sub("", text)
180	    if cap is not None and len(text) > cap:
181	        text = text[:cap]
182	    return text
183	
184	
185	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
186	# Conclusion dedup — normalised text form.
187	#
188	# The Codex DB has a `Conclusion.normalizedText` column populated by this
189	# helper and matched by a `(organizationId, normalizedText)` index. Any
190	# time this pipeline is about to write a new Conclusion we first look up
191	# an existing row with the same normalised key in the same org; if one
192	# exists we skip the INSERT and add this upload as an extra source via
193	# `ConclusionSource`. That's what makes deletion work correctly — a
194	# Conclusion whose one-and-only source is removed gets orphan-deleted,
195	# while a Conclusion supported by several uploads stays in place with
196	# the surviving uploads as its remaining sources.
197	#
198	# The formula (lower + strip + collapse whitespace) matches the SQL
199	# backfill in
200	# `prisma/migrations/20260420000000_conclusion_sources_and_derived_cascade`,
201	# so pre-existing rows and freshly-inserted rows share the same key.
202	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
203	
204	_WS_COLLAPSE_RE = re.compile(r"\s+")
205	
206	
207	def _normalize_claim_text(text: str) -> str:
208	    """Return the dedup key for a claim. Empty input → empty key."""
209	    if not text:
210	        return ""
211	    return _WS_COLLAPSE_RE.sub(" ", text).strip().lower()
212	
213	
214	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
215	# Naive claim extraction — works without an LLM. Conservative: keeps only
216	# sentences that look like first-person / declarative assertions, filters
217	# questions and fragments. Good enough for a first-pass Codex display.
218	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
219	
220	
221	# Sentence-level tokens ending with a period / exclamation / line break.
222	# Non-greedy so we don't over-fuse sentences across paragraph breaks.
223	_SENT_RE = re.compile(r"[^.!?\n]+[.!?]+|[^.!?\n]+(?=\n|$)", re.MULTILINE)
224	
225	# Heuristic filters for sentences we SHOULDN'T promote to claims.
226	_QUESTION_RE = re.compile(r"\?\s*$")
227	_LIST_BULLET_RE = re.compile(r"^\s*[-*•\d]+[\.\)]?\s+")
228	_URL_RE = re.compile(r"https?://|www\.")
229	_HEADER_RE = re.compile(r"^#+\s|^={3,}\s*$|^-{3,}\s*$")
230	
231	
232	@dataclass
233	class NaiveClaim:
234	    text: str
235	    claim_type: str = "empirical"  # conservative default
236	
237	
238	def naive_extract_claims(text: str, *, max_claims: int = 40) -> list[NaiveClaim]:
239	    """
240	    Sentence-split a blob of text and keep only sentences that look like
241	    substantive claims:
242	      * length ≥ 40 chars (filters "Yes.", "Ok.", lone bullets)
243	      * length ≤ 400 chars (filters run-on paragraphs that aren't atomic)
244	      * not a question (``?`` at end)
245	      * not a list bullet / markdown header
246	      * contains at least one verb-like word (``is|are|was|were|should|must|can|may|will``)
247	        OR a contextual connector (``because|therefore|given|since``)
248	
249	    Returns at most ``max_claims`` so a 50-page transcript doesn't blow
250	    up the Codex with thousands of rows.
251	    """
252	    out: list[NaiveClaim] = []
253	    cleaned = _HEADER_RE.sub("", text)
254	    # Normalise whitespace so sentence splitting doesn't fragment on line
255	    # wraps inside a paragraph. We preserve paragraph breaks (double-newline)
256	    # because they are a useful signal for separating assertions; we just
257	    # rejoin line wraps WITHIN a paragraph into spaces.
258	    cleaned = re.sub(r"(?<!\n)\n(?!\n)", " ", cleaned)
259	    cleaned = re.sub(r"[ \t]+", " ", cleaned)
260	    # Walk sentences.
261	    for match in _SENT_RE.finditer(cleaned):
262	        raw = match.group(0).strip()
263	        if not raw:
264	            continue
265	        if len(raw) < 40 or len(raw) > 400:
266	            continue
267	        if _QUESTION_RE.search(raw):
268	            continue
269	        if _LIST_BULLET_RE.match(raw):
270	            # Strip the bullet and re-check length.
271	            stripped = _LIST_BULLET_RE.sub("", raw).strip()
272	            if len(stripped) < 40 or len(stripped) > 400:
273	                continue
274	            raw = stripped
275	        if _URL_RE.search(raw):
276	            # Drop sentences that are mostly URLs — they're rarely claims.
277	            continue
278	        lower = raw.lower()
279	        has_verb = bool(
280	            re.search(
281	                r"\b(is|are|was|were|should|must|can|may|will|would|do|does|did|have|has|had|seems|appears|means|implies|suggests|shows|proves|causes|requires|matters)\b",
282	                lower,
283	            )
284	        )
285	        has_connector = bool(
286	            re.search(r"\b(because|therefore|thus|hence|since|given that)\b", lower)
287	        )
288	        if not (has_verb or has_connector):
289	            continue
290	        # Classify: rough taxonomy based on vocabulary.
291	        claim_type = "empirical"
292	        if re.search(r"\b(should|must|ought|right|wrong|better|worse|good|bad)\b", lower):
293	            claim_type = "normative"
294	        elif re.search(
295	            r"\b(method|framework|approach|process|procedure|methodology)\b", lower
296	        ):
297	            claim_type = "methodological"
298	        elif re.search(r"\b(will|won't|shall|going to|expect|predict)\b", lower):
299	            claim_type = "predictive"
300	        out.append(NaiveClaim(text=raw, claim_type=claim_type))
301	        if len(out) >= max_claims:
302	            break
303	    return out
304	
305	
306	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
307	# LLM-backed extraction — uses Noosphere's registered ``extract_claims`` method.
308	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
309	
310	
311	def llm_extract_claims(
312	    text: str,
313	    *,
314	    chunk_char_size: int = 3500,
315	    max_chunks: int = 10,
316	    upload_id: str = "upload",
317	) -> list[NaiveClaim]:
318	    """LLM-backed extraction via Noosphere's ``extract_claims`` method.
319	
320	    Raises if the LLM is unconfigured — the caller is expected to have
321	    passed --with-llm deliberately.
322	    """
323	    from noosphere.methods.extract_claims import (
324	        ExtractClaimsInput,
325	        extract_claims,
326	    )
327	
328	    # Chunk the text so no single LLM call blows past the context budget.
329	    # Paragraph splits preferred; fall back to hard-cut if a paragraph is
330	    # itself huge.
331	    raw_paras = re.split(r"\n\s*\n+", text)
332	    chunks: list[str] = []
333	    buf: list[str] = []
334	    size = 0
335	    for p in raw_paras:
336	        if size + len(p) > chunk_char_size and buf:
337	            chunks.append("\n\n".join(buf))
338	            buf = []
339	            size = 0
340	        if len(p) > chunk_char_size:
341	            # Hard-split the monster paragraph.
342	            for i in range(0, len(p), chunk_char_size):
343	                chunks.append(p[i : i + chunk_char_size])
344	        else:
345	            buf.append(p)
346	            size += len(p) + 2
347	    if buf:
348	        chunks.append("\n\n".join(buf))
349	
350	    chunks = chunks[:max_chunks]
351	
352	    out: list[NaiveClaim] = []
353	    for i, chunk in enumerate(chunks):
354	        result = extract_claims(
355	            ExtractClaimsInput(
356	                chunk_text=chunk,
357	                chunk_id=f"{upload_id}_chunk_{i}",
358	                episode_id=upload_id,
359	                speaker_name="founder",
360	                speaker_role="founder",
361	            )
362	        )
363	        for c in result.claims:
364	            if not c.text.strip():
365	                continue
366	            # Drop claims the LLM flagged as external (interview prompts,
367	            # counter-positions, quoted views) so they never land in the
368	            # founder's Conclusion canon. This is defense-in-depth: the
369	            # extractor already labels them, the bridge refuses to
370	            # propagate them.
371	            if getattr(c, "is_author_assertion", True) is False:
372	                continue
373	            out.append(NaiveClaim(text=c.text.strip(), claim_type=c.claim_type))
374	    return out
375	
376	
377	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
378	# Core pipeline — connects to Codex Postgres, reads upload, writes results.
379	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
380	
381	
382	@dataclass
383	class IngestFromCodexResult:
384	    upload_id: str
385	    title: str
386	    num_claims_extracted: int
387	    num_conclusions_written: int
388	    num_contradictions_written: int
389	    num_open_questions_written: int
390	    num_research_suggestions_written: int
391	    dry_run: bool
392	    mode: str  # "naive" | "llm"
393	
394	
395	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
396	# Contradiction detection — naive (always runs) + LLM-augmented (optional).
397	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
398	
399	
400	@dataclass
401	class DetectedContradiction:
402	    """A contradicting claim pair. `a_idx` / `b_idx` refer to indices in
403	    the claim list we just extracted; if one side is an existing firm
404	    Conclusion (cross-upload contradiction) we use `b_existing_id` instead
405	    of `b_idx`."""
406	    a_idx: int
407	    b_idx: int | None  # None when b is an existing firm Conclusion
408	    b_existing_id: str | None  # Conclusion.id when b is from the firm corpus
409	    severity: float   # 0..1
410	    narrative: str
411	    source: str       # "heuristic" | "llm"
412	
413	
414	_NEGATION_TOKENS = re.compile(
415	    r"\b(not|never|no|none|nothing|nobody|nowhere|n'?t|cannot|can'?t|won'?t|"
416	    r"shouldn'?t|wouldn'?t|couldn'?t|isn'?t|aren'?t|wasn'?t|weren'?t|"
417	    r"doesn'?t|didn'?t|don'?t|haven'?t|hasn'?t|hadn'?t)\b",
418	    flags=re.IGNORECASE,
419	)
420	_STOPWORDS = {
421	    "the", "a", "an", "is", "are", "was", "were", "be", "been", "being",
422	    "and", "or", "but", "if", "then", "of", "in", "on", "at", "to", "for",
423	    "by", "with", "from", "as", "that", "this", "these", "those", "it",
424	    "its", "we", "our", "you", "they", "them", "their", "i", "me", "my",
425	    "he", "she", "his", "her", "has", "have", "had", "do", "does", "did",
426	    "will", "would", "should", "could", "can", "may", "must", "ought",
427	    "so", "than", "such", "some", "any", "all", "every", "each", "both",
428	    "more", "most", "much", "many", "few",
429	}
430	
431	
432	def _content_tokens(text: str) -> set[str]:
433	    toks = re.findall(r"\b[a-z][a-z']+\b", text.lower())
434	    return {t for t in toks if t not in _STOPWORDS and len(t) >= 4}
435	
436	
437	def _has_negation(text: str) -> bool:
438	    return bool(_NEGATION_TOKENS.search(text))
439	
440	
441	def naive_detect_contradictions(
442	    claims: list[NaiveClaim],
443	) -> list[DetectedContradiction]:
444	    """
445	    Heuristic pairwise contradiction scan on the new claims. Flags pairs
446	    where:
447	      (1) their content vocabulary overlaps significantly (same subject), AND
448	      (2) exactly one contains a negation marker (opposite polarity).
449	
450	    This catches the obvious "X should always …" vs "X should not …" case
451	    without needing an NLI model. Recall is modest; precision is decent
452	    enough that the flagged pairs are usually worth a human's attention.
453	    """
454	    out: list[DetectedContradiction] = []
455	    tok_sets = [_content_tokens(c.text) for c in claims]
456	    neg_flags = [_has_negation(c.text) for c in claims]
457	    for i in range(len(claims)):
458	        for j in range(i + 1, len(claims)):
459	            if neg_flags[i] == neg_flags[j]:
460	                continue  # both affirmative or both negative — no polarity flip
461	            if not tok_sets[i] or not tok_sets[j]:
462	                continue
463	            shared = tok_sets[i] & tok_sets[j]
464	            # Require meaningful overlap — otherwise it's just two unrelated
465	            # sentences that happen to have different polarity. A Jaccard
466	            # of 0.35 on content tokens is a useful middle ground.
467	            smaller = min(len(tok_sets[i]), len(tok_sets[j]))
468	            if smaller == 0 or (len(shared) / smaller) < 0.35:
469	                continue
470	            # Severity proxy: how much do they overlap? More overlap = more
471	            # directly contradictory.
472	            overlap_ratio = len(shared) / smaller
473	            severity = min(0.9, 0.4 + overlap_ratio * 0.5)
474	            out.append(
475	                DetectedContradiction(
476	                    a_idx=i,
477	                    b_idx=j,
478	                    b_existing_id=None,
479	                    severity=severity,
480	                    narrative=(
481	                        f"Heuristic: shared vocabulary {sorted(shared)[:6]} "
482	                        f"but opposite polarity."
483	                    ),
484	                    source="heuristic",
485	                )
486	            )
487	    return out
488	
489	
490	def llm_detect_contradictions_and_questions(
491	    claims: list[NaiveClaim],
492	    firm_conclusions: list[tuple[str, str]],  # (id, text) of existing firm claims
493	    *,
494	    upload_title: str,
495	) -> dict:
496	    """
497	    Single LLM call that does all three analysis tasks at once —
498	    contradictions, open questions, research suggestions. Putting them in
499	    one prompt is:
500	      (a) cheaper (single context load);
501	      (b) more coherent — the questions are generated in light of the
502	          contradictions the LLM just identified, instead of being
503	          independently invented.
504	
505	    Returns a dict shaped like:
506	      {
507	        "contradictions": [{"a": int, "b": int, "severity": float, "narrative": str}],
508	        "cross_contradictions": [{"a": int, "existing_id": str, "severity": float, "narrative": str}],
509	        "open_questions": [{"summary": str, "unresolved_reason": str, "a": int, "b": int|None}],
510	        "research_suggestions": [{"title": str, "summary": str, "rationale": str}],
511	      }
512	    Missing keys are backfilled to empty lists so the caller never
513	    explodes on a partial response.
514	
515	    Raises on LLM failure so the caller can decide whether to fall back
516	    to heuristic-only mode.
517	    """
518	    from noosphere.llm import llm_client_from_settings
519	
520	    llm = llm_client_from_settings()
521	
522	    new_lines = "\n".join(
523	        f"  [{i}] {c.text}" for i, c in enumerate(claims)
524	    )
525	    firm_lines = (
526	        "\n".join(f"  <{fid[:10]}> {txt[:240]}" for fid, txt in firm_conclusions[:40])
527	        if firm_conclusions
528	        else "  (none yet — this is the first upload for this firm.)"
529	    )
530	
531	    system = (
532	        "You are an analyst helping a firm surface contradictions, open "
533	        "questions, and research directions from a newly uploaded document. "
534	        "Answer ONLY in JSON matching the schema below. Be conservative — "
535	        "only flag genuine contradictions, not mere topical overlap. Only "
536	        "propose questions a founder could actually research. Never "
537	        "fabricate evidence."
538	    )
539	    user = f"""New document: "{upload_title}"
540	
541	CLAIMS extracted from this document (indices in brackets):
542	{new_lines}
543	
544	FIRM'S EXISTING CONCLUSIONS (for cross-upload contradiction detection):
545	{firm_lines}
546	
547	Return a single JSON object with these keys:
548	{{
549	  "contradictions": [
550	    {{"a": <idx>, "b": <idx>, "severity": <0..1>, "narrative": "<≤200 chars, why they conflict>"}}
551	  ],
552	  "cross_contradictions": [
553	    {{"a": <idx>, "existing_id": "<existing firm claim id prefix>", "severity": <0..1>, "narrative": "<≤200 chars>"}}
554	  ],
555	  "open_questions": [
556	    {{"summary": "<≤180 chars, a concrete question>", "unresolved_reason": "<why unresolved>", "a": <idx or null>, "b": <idx or null>}}
557	  ],
558	  "research_suggestions": [
559	    {{"title": "<short title>", "summary": "<one sentence>", "rationale": "<why this matters>"}}
560	  ]
561	}}
562	
563	Rules:
564	- 0-5 contradictions, 0-3 cross_contradictions, 3-6 open_questions, 2-4 research_suggestions.
565	- If no real contradictions exist, return [] for that list. Do not pad.
566	- `a` and `b` must be valid indices into the CLAIMS list above.
567	- Output ONLY the JSON object, no prose."""
568	
569	    raw = llm.complete(system=system, user=user, max_tokens=2500)
570	    m = re.search(r"\{.*\}", raw, re.DOTALL)
571	    if not m:
572	        raise RuntimeError(f"LLM returned non-JSON: {raw[:200]}")
573	    payload = json.loads(m.group(0))
574	    return {
575	        "contradictions": payload.get("contradictions", []) or [],
576	        "cross_contradictions": payload.get("cross_contradictions", []) or [],
577	        "open_questions": payload.get("open_questions", []) or [],
578	        "research_suggestions": payload.get("research_suggestions", []) or [],
579	    }
580	
581	
582	def _resolve_codex_db_url(explicit: Optional[str]) -> str:
583	    if explicit:
584	        return explicit
585	    for env in (
586	        "THESEUS_CODEX_DATABASE_URL",
587	        "CODEX_DATABASE_URL",
588	        "DIRECT_URL",
589	        "DATABASE_URL",
590	    ):
591	        val = os.environ.get(env, "").strip()
592	        if val:
593	            return val
594	    raise RuntimeError(
595	        "No Codex Postgres URL found. Pass --codex-db-url, or export "
596	        "THESEUS_CODEX_DATABASE_URL / CODEX_DATABASE_URL / DIRECT_URL / "
597	        "DATABASE_URL. The Supabase DIRECT_URL (port 5432, not the 6543 "
598	        "pooler) is preferred — the pooler can strip advisory locks some "
599	        "ORMs rely on."
600	    )
601	
602	
603	def _short_cuid_like() -> str:
604	    """Simple unique id for Conclusion / AuditEvent rows. Prisma normally
605	    uses cuid() but the DB only enforces uniqueness, so a uuid4-derived
606	    id works fine and keeps this file Python-only (no cuid dep)."""
607	    return "c_" + uuid.uuid4().hex[:24]
608	
609	
610	def ingest_from_codex(
611	    upload_id: str,
612	    *,
613	    codex_db_url: Optional[str] = None,
614	    use_llm: bool = False,
615	    max_claims: int = 40,
616	    dry_run: bool = False,
617	    organization_slug_filter: Optional[str] = None,
618	) -> IngestFromCodexResult:
619	    """
620	    Read a Codex Upload, extract claims, write Conclusions back into the
621	    same DB. Returns a small result object so the CLI can print a summary.
622	    """
623	    url = _resolve_codex_db_url(codex_db_url)
624	    conn = _open_codex_connection(url)
625	    try:
626	        cur = conn.cursor(cursor_factory=psycopg2.extras.RealDictCursor)
627	
628	        # ── Fetch the upload ─────────────────────────────────────────────
629	        cur.execute(
630	            '''SELECT id, "organizationId", "founderId", title, "textContent",
631	                      status, "mimeType", "originalName", "filePath", "fileSize"
632	               FROM "Upload" WHERE id = %s''',
633	            (upload_id,),
634	        )
635	        row = cur.fetchone()
636	        if row is None:
637	            raise RuntimeError(f"Upload {upload_id} not found in the Codex DB.")
638	
639	        # ── Fetch-and-extract via the MIME dispatcher ───────────────────
640	        # ``fetch_upload_content`` returns TextContent when the DB has
641	        # textContent populated, or BinaryContent (bytes + mime) when the
642	        # row points at Supabase Storage / a local path. ``dispatch``
643	        # then routes to the appropriate extractor (passthrough for text,
644	        # Whisper for audio — see prompt 02, pypdf for PDF — see prompt
645	        # 03). Failures are marked on the Upload row with a structured
646	        # reason so the dashboard (prompt 04) can surface them.
647	        from noosphere.extractors import (
648	            ExtractionFailed,
649	            UnsupportedMimeType,
650	            dispatch,
651	        )
652	        from noosphere.extractors._content_fetcher import fetch_upload_content
653	
654	        # Mark `extracting` as we enter the MIME dispatch. This is the
655	        # stage where Whisper/pypdf run; the Codex dashboard shows an
656	        # amber pulsing badge so the founder can see the pipeline is
657	        # alive before any LLM calls happen.
658	        if not dry_run:
659	            cur.execute(
660	                'UPDATE "Upload" SET status = %s WHERE id = %s',
661	                ("extracting", upload_id),
662	            )
663	            conn.commit()
664	
665	        try:
666	            content = fetch_upload_content(row, conn)
667	            extracted = dispatch(content)
668	        except (ExtractionFailed, UnsupportedMimeType) as exc:
669	            reason = (
670	                f"unsupported_mime: {exc}"
671	                if isinstance(exc, UnsupportedMimeType)
672	                else f"extraction_failed: {exc}"
673	            )
674	            if not dry_run:
675	                cur.execute(
676	                    '''UPDATE "Upload"
677	                       SET status = %s, "errorMessage" = %s
678	                       WHERE id = %s''',
679	                    ("failed", _db_safe(reason, cap=400), upload_id),
680	                )
681	                conn.commit()
682	            raise
683	
684	        text = extracted.text
685	        print(
686	            f"extraction method={extracted.extraction_method} "
687	            f"source_format={extracted.source_format} "
688	            f"warnings={len(extracted.warnings)}"
689	        )
690	        for w in extracted.warnings:
691	            print(f"  warning: {w}")
692	
693	        if not text or not text.strip():
694	            raise RuntimeError(
695	                f"Upload {upload_id}: extractor {extracted.extraction_method!r} "
696	                "returned empty text."
697	            )
698	
699	        # Optional sanity check on tenant if the caller passed a slug — protects
700	        # against running this against the wrong Codex tenant by mistake.
701	        if organization_slug_filter:
702	            cur.execute(
703	                'SELECT slug FROM "Organization" WHERE id = %s',
704	                (row["organizationId"],),
705	            )
706	            org = cur.fetchone()
707	            if not org or org["slug"] != organization_slug_filter:
708	                raise RuntimeError(
709	                    f"Upload {upload_id} belongs to org {org['slug'] if org else '?'}, "
710	                    f"not the expected slug '{organization_slug_filter}'."
711	                )
712	
713	        # ── Intermediate beat: text is ready, claim extraction next ──────
714	        # The dashboard shows `awaiting_ingest` momentarily between the
715	        # two heavy stages. For uploads with no PromptSeparator/LLM work
716	        # this flashes by — the point is that if the pipeline stalls,
717	        # the stall point is visible.
718	        if not dry_run:
719	            cur.execute(
720	                'UPDATE "Upload" SET status = %s WHERE id = %s',
721	                ("awaiting_ingest", upload_id),
722	            )
723	            conn.commit()
724	
725	        # ── Mark processing (non-dry only) ───────────────────────────────
726	        if not dry_run:
727	            cur.execute(
728	                'UPDATE "Upload" SET status = %s WHERE id = %s',
729	                ("processing", upload_id),
730	            )
731	            conn.commit()
732	
733	        # ── Strip interview prompts / external quotes BEFORE extraction ─
734	        # Defense-in-depth against misattributing external text as
735	        # founder claims. Failures here log & fall through to raw text.
736	        try:
737	            from noosphere.mitigations.prompt_separator import PromptSeparator
738	            separated = PromptSeparator().separate(text, source_type="written")
739	            print(
740	                f"prompt_separator founder_sections={len(separated.founder_sections)} "
741	                f"prompt_sections={len(separated.prompt_sections)} "
742	                f"confidence={separated.confidence:.2f}"
743	            )
744	            if separated.founder_sections:
745	                text = separated.founder_text
746	        except Exception as sep_err:
747	            print(f"prompt_separator_failed: {sep_err}")
748	
749	        # ── Extract claims ───────────────────────────────────────────────
750	        if use_llm:
751	            claims = llm_extract_claims(text, upload_id=upload_id, max_chunks=10)
752	            mode = "llm"
753	        else:
754	            claims = naive_extract_claims(text, max_claims=max_claims)
755	            mode = "naive"
756	
757	        if dry_run:
758	            return IngestFromCodexResult(
759	                upload_id=upload_id,
760	                title=row["title"] or row["originalName"] or upload_id,
761	                num_claims_extracted=len(claims),
762	                num_conclusions_written=0,
763	                num_contradictions_written=0,
764	                num_open_questions_written=0,
765	                num_research_suggestions_written=0,
766	                dry_run=True,
767	                mode=mode,
768	            )
769	
770	        # ── Insert conclusions (with dedup + source linking) ─────────────
771	        # We also track a parallel list of (claim_index → conclusion_id)
772	        # so we can cite the right Conclusion rows from contradictions
773	        # and open questions below. Claims that are too short to insert
774	        # won't appear in this map (their index maps to None).
775	        #
776	        # Dedup pass: before inserting a Conclusion we look for an
777	        # existing row in this org with the same normalised text. If one
778	        # exists, we DON'T duplicate — we just add this upload as an
779	        # additional source via ConclusionSource. That's the mechanic
780	        # that lets a Conclusion survive when one of its two sources is
781	        # later deleted. A fresh insert writes both the Conclusion AND a
782	        # ConclusionSource row.
783	        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
784	        written = 0  # NEW conclusions inserted
785	        deduped = 0  # claims that matched an existing conclusion
786	        claim_idx_to_conclusion_id: list[str | None] = [None] * len(claims)
787	        # Pre-compute a sanitized source label we'll reuse in every row's
788	        # rationale/sessionLabel. Cap at 300 so a giant filename can't blow
789	        # the text column.
790	        source_label = _db_safe(
791	            row["title"] or row["originalName"] or upload_id,
792	            cap=300,
793	        )
794	        for idx, claim in enumerate(claims):
795	            claim_text = _db_safe(claim.text, cap=4_000).strip()
796	            if len(claim_text) < 20:
797	                continue
798	            normalized = _normalize_claim_text(claim_text)
799	            if not normalized:
800	                continue
801	
802	            # Dedup lookup — matching is scoped to the same org.
803	            cur.execute(
804	                '''SELECT id FROM "Conclusion"
805	                   WHERE "organizationId" = %s AND "normalizedText" = %s
806	                   LIMIT 1''',
807	                (row["organizationId"], normalized),
808	            )
809	            existing = cur.fetchone()
810	
811	            if existing:
812	                cid = existing["id"]
813	                # Attach this upload as another source. ON CONFLICT is
814	                # a defensive no-op in case the user is re-running the
815	                # ingest for the same upload.
816	                cur.execute(
817	                    '''INSERT INTO "ConclusionSource"
818	                       ("conclusionId", "uploadId", "createdAt")
819	                       VALUES (%s, %s, %s)
820	                       ON CONFLICT ("conclusionId", "uploadId") DO NOTHING''',
821	                    (cid, upload_id, now),
822	                )
823	                claim_idx_to_conclusion_id[idx] = cid
824	                deduped += 1
825	                continue
826	
827	            cid = _short_cuid_like()
828	            noosphere_id = f"ing_{upload_id}_{idx}"
829	            cur.execute(
830	                '''INSERT INTO "Conclusion"
831	                   (id, "organizationId", "noosphereId", text, "normalizedText",
832	                    "confidenceTier", rationale, "supportingPrincipleIds",
833	                    "evidenceChainClaimIds", "dissentClaimIds",
834	                    confidence, "topicHint", "createdAt")
835	                   VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s)
836	                   ON CONFLICT ("noosphereId") DO NOTHING''',
837	                (
838	                    cid,
839	                    row["organizationId"],
840	                    noosphere_id,
841	                    claim_text,
842	                    normalized,
843	                    "open",
844	                    _db_safe(f"Extracted from upload: {source_label}", cap=500),
845	                    json.dumps([]),
846	                    json.dumps([]),
847	                    json.dumps([]),
848	                    0.5,
849	                    _db_safe(claim.claim_type, cap=64),
850	                    now,
851	                ),
852	            )
853	            if cur.rowcount == 1:
854	                written += 1
855	                claim_idx_to_conclusion_id[idx] = cid
856	                cur.execute(
857	                    '''INSERT INTO "ConclusionSource"
858	                       ("conclusionId", "uploadId", "createdAt")
859	                       VALUES (%s, %s, %s)
860	                       ON CONFLICT ("conclusionId", "uploadId") DO NOTHING''',
861	                    (cid, upload_id, now),
862	                )
863	
864	        # ── Contradiction detection ─────────────────────────────────────
865	        # Always run the heuristic pass (cheap, no deps). If LLM is
866	        # available AND use_llm=True, do the combined contradictions +
867	        # questions + research pass for higher quality.
868	        contradictions_written = 0
869	        open_questions_written = 0
870	        research_suggestions_written = 0
871	
872	        heuristic_pairs = naive_detect_contradictions(claims)
873	
874	        llm_payload: dict | None = None
875	        llm_error: str | None = None
876	        if use_llm:
877	            try:
878	                # Pull up to 40 recent firm conclusions for cross-upload checking.
879	                cur.execute(
880	                    '''SELECT id, text FROM "Conclusion"
881	                       WHERE "organizationId" = %s
882	                         AND "noosphereId" NOT LIKE %s
883	                       ORDER BY "createdAt" DESC LIMIT 40''',
884	                    (row["organizationId"], f"ing_{upload_id}_%"),
885	                )
886	                firm_conclusions = [(r["id"], r["text"]) for r in cur.fetchall()]
887	                llm_payload = llm_detect_contradictions_and_questions(
888	                    claims,
889	                    firm_conclusions,
890	                    upload_title=row["title"] or row["originalName"] or upload_id,
891	                )
892	            except Exception as exc:
893	                # LLM failure is non-fatal — we still have the heuristic
894	                # contradictions and the inserted Conclusions. Record the
895	                # reason so the caller can surface it in the process log.
896	                llm_error = f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
897	
898	        # ── Write contradictions (both sources merged) ──────────────────
899	        # Every contradiction carries `sourceUploadId = upload_id` — it's
900	        # the product of THIS ingestion run. Cross-upload contradictions
901	        # (where claim B is an existing firm Conclusion from a DIFFERENT
902	        # upload) also get the current upload as their source, since that
903	        # upload is the one that surfaced the conflict. Deleting either
904	        # upload will remove the contradiction — the current upload via
905	        # its `sourceUploadId`, the other upload via claimBId being
906	        # orphan-deleted when ITS source Conclusion loses all sources.
907	        for pair in heuristic_pairs:
908	            a_cid = claim_idx_to_conclusion_id[pair.a_idx]
909	            b_cid = (
910	                claim_idx_to_conclusion_id[pair.b_idx]
911	                if pair.b_idx is not None
912	                else pair.b_existing_id
913	            )
914	            if not a_cid or not b_cid:
915	                continue
916	            cur.execute(
917	                '''INSERT INTO "Contradiction"
918	                   (id, "organizationId", "claimAId", "claimBId", severity,
919	                    "sixLayerJson", narrative, "sourceUploadId", "createdAt")
920	                   VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s)''',
921	                (
922	                    "x_" + uuid.uuid4().hex[:24],
923	                    row["organizationId"],
924	                    a_cid,
925	                    b_cid,
926	                    float(pair.severity),
927	                    json.dumps({"source": "heuristic"}),
928	                    _db_safe(pair.narrative, cap=2_000),
929	                    upload_id,
930	                    now,
931	                ),
932	            )
933	            contradictions_written += cur.rowcount
934	
935	        # LLM contradictions (within-upload).
936	        if llm_payload:
937	            for item in llm_payload.get("contradictions", []):
938	                try:
939	                    a = int(item["a"])
940	                    b = int(item["b"])
941	                    sev = float(item.get("severity", 0.6))
942	                    narrative = str(item.get("narrative", ""))[:500]
943	                except (KeyError, ValueError, TypeError):
944	                    continue
945	                a_cid = claim_idx_to_conclusion_id[a] if 0 <= a < len(claims) else None
946	                b_cid = claim_idx_to_conclusion_id[b] if 0 <= b < len(claims) else None
947	                if not a_cid or not b_cid or a_cid == b_cid:
948	                    continue
949	                cur.execute(
950	                    '''INSERT INTO "Contradiction"
951	                       (id, "organizationId", "claimAId", "claimBId", severity,
952	                        "sixLayerJson", narrative, "sourceUploadId", "createdAt")
953	                       VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s)''',
954	                    (
955	                        "x_" + uuid.uuid4().hex[:24],
956	                        row["organizationId"],
957	                        a_cid,
958	                        b_cid,
959	                        sev,
960	                        json.dumps({"source": "llm"}),
961	                        _db_safe(narrative or "LLM flagged as contradictory", cap=2_000),
962	                        upload_id,
963	                        now,
964	                    ),
965	                )
966	                contradictions_written += cur.rowcount
967	
968	            # Cross-upload contradictions (new claim vs existing firm claim).
969	            # The LLM returns a prefix like "abc1234…"; we match by prefix
970	            # against firm_conclusions' full ids.
971	            firm_id_lookup = {fid[:10]: fid for fid, _ in firm_conclusions}
972	            for item in llm_payload.get("cross_contradictions", []):
973	                try:
974	                    a = int(item["a"])
975	                    existing_prefix = str(item.get("existing_id", ""))[:10]
976	                    sev = float(item.get("severity", 0.6))
977	                    narrative = str(item.get("narrative", ""))[:500]
978	                except (KeyError, ValueError, TypeError):
979	                    continue
980	                a_cid = claim_idx_to_conclusion_id[a] if 0 <= a < len(claims) else None
981	                existing_id = firm_id_lookup.get(existing_prefix)
982	                if not a_cid or not existing_id or a_cid == existing_id:
983	                    continue
984	                cur.execute(
985	                    '''INSERT INTO "Contradiction"
986	                       (id, "organizationId", "claimAId", "claimBId", severity,
987	                        "sixLayerJson", narrative, "sourceUploadId", "createdAt")
988	                       VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s)''',
989	                    (
990	                        "x_" + uuid.uuid4().hex[:24],
991	                        row["organizationId"],
992	                        a_cid,
993	                        existing_id,
994	                        sev,
995	                        json.dumps({"source": "llm", "cross_upload": True}),
996	                        _db_safe(narrative or "LLM cross-upload contradiction", cap=2_000),
997	                        upload_id,
998	                        now,
999	                    ),
1000	                )
1001	                contradictions_written += cur.rowcount
1002	
1003	        # ── Write open questions (LLM only) ─────────────────────────────
1004	        if llm_payload:
1005	            for item in llm_payload.get("open_questions", []):
1006	                try:
1007	                    summary = str(item.get("summary", "")).strip()[:500]
1008	                    reason = str(item.get("unresolved_reason", "")).strip()[:500]
1009	                except (KeyError, TypeError):
1010	                    continue
1011	                if not summary:
1012	                    continue
1013	                a = item.get("a")
1014	                b = item.get("b")
1015	                a_cid = (
1016	                    claim_idx_to_conclusion_id[int(a)]
1017	                    if isinstance(a, int) and 0 <= a < len(claims)
1018	                    else None
1019	                )
1020	                b_cid = (
1021	                    claim_idx_to_conclusion_id[int(b)]
1022	                    if isinstance(b, int) and 0 <= b < len(claims)
1023	                    else None
1024	                )
1025	                # OpenQuestion requires claimAId + claimBId NOT NULL; if the
1026	                # LLM didn't cite any, anchor both to the first inserted
1027	                # conclusion as a minimal placeholder so the row is valid.
1028	                # A future schema migration could make these nullable.
1029	                fallback = next(
1030	                    (cid for cid in claim_idx_to_conclusion_id if cid is not None),
1031	                    None,
1032	                )
1033	                a_cid = a_cid or fallback
1034	                b_cid = b_cid or fallback
1035	                if not a_cid or not b_cid:
1036	                    continue
1037	                cur.execute(
1038	                    '''INSERT INTO "OpenQuestion"
1039	                       (id, "organizationId", "noosphereId", summary,
1040	                        "claimAId", "claimBId", "unresolvedReason",
1041	                        "layerDisagreementSummary", "sourceUploadId",
1042	                        "createdAt")
1043	                       VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s)''',
1044	                    (
1045	                        "q_" + uuid.uuid4().hex[:24],
1046	                        row["organizationId"],
1047	                        f"oq_{upload_id}_{open_questions_written}",
1048	                        _db_safe(summary, cap=2_000),
1049	                        a_cid,
1050	                        b_cid,
1051	                        _db_safe(reason or "Surfaced during ingest LLM pass", cap=2_000),
1052	                        "LLM-flagged",
1053	                        upload_id,
1054	                        now,
1055	                    ),
1056	                )
1057	                open_questions_written += cur.rowcount
1058	
1059	        # ── Write research suggestions (LLM only) ───────────────────────
1060	        if llm_payload:
1061	            for item in llm_payload.get("research_suggestions", []):
1062	                try:
1063	                    title = str(item.get("title", "")).strip()[:300]
1064	                    summary = str(item.get("summary", "")).strip()[:1000]
1065	                    rationale = str(item.get("rationale", "")).strip()[:1000]
1066	                except (KeyError, TypeError):
1067	                    continue
1068	                if not title or not summary:
1069	                    continue
1070	                cur.execute(
1071	                    '''INSERT INTO "ResearchSuggestion"
1072	                       (id, "organizationId", "noosphereId", title, summary,
1073	                        rationale, "readingUris", "sessionLabel",
1074	                        "suggestedForFounderId", "sourceUploadId", "createdAt")
1075	                       VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s)''',
1076	                    (
1077	                        "rs_" + uuid.uuid4().hex[:24],
1078	                        row["organizationId"],
1079	                        f"rs_{upload_id}_{research_suggestions_written}",
1080	                        _db_safe(title, cap=300),
1081	                        _db_safe(summary, cap=2_000),
1082	                        _db_safe(rationale or "LLM-generated during ingest", cap=2_000),
1083	                        json.dumps([]),
1084	                        source_label,
1085	                        row["founderId"],
1086	                        upload_id,
1087	                        now,
1088	                    ),
1089	                )
1090	                research_suggestions_written += cur.rowcount
1091	
1092	        # ── Update upload row ────────────────────────────────────────────
1093	        # claimsCount reflects BOTH newly-inserted conclusions AND ones
1094	        # this upload re-sourced (via dedup) — the number of distinct
1095	        # claims this upload anchors, not just the fresh inserts. That
1096	        # matches the user-facing "N claims extracted" semantics better
1097	        # than a raw INSERT count would.
1098	        # `extractionMethod` is the caption the Codex dashboard shows
1099	        # under an ingested row ("transcribed locally (faster-whisper)",
1100	        # "extracted with pypdf"…). Written on the same UPDATE as the
1101	        # terminal status so a partial success leaves a consistent row.
1102	        cur.execute(
1103	            '''UPDATE "Upload"
1104	               SET status = %s,
1105	                   "claimsCount" = %s,
1106	                   "errorMessage" = NULL,
1107	                   "extractionMethod" = %s
1108	               WHERE id = %s''',
1109	            (
1110	                "ingested",
1111	                written + deduped,
1112	                _db_safe(extracted.extraction_method, cap=64),
1113	                upload_id,
1114	            ),
1115	        )
1116	
1117	        # ── Audit event ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
1118	        audit_detail = (
1119	            f"Noosphere {mode} ingest · "
1120	            f"{written} new conclusions · "
1121	            f"{deduped} re-sourced · "
1122	            f"{contradictions_written} contradictions · "
1123	            f"{open_questions_written} open questions · "
1124	            f"{research_suggestions_written} research suggestions"
1125	        )
1126	        if llm_error:
1127	            audit_detail += f" · LLM pass failed: {llm_error[:80]}"
1128	        cur.execute(
1129	            '''INSERT INTO "AuditEvent"
1130	               (id, "organizationId", "founderId", "uploadId", action, detail, "createdAt")
1131	               VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s)''',
1132	            (
1133	                "ae_" + uuid.uuid4().hex[:24],
1134	                row["organizationId"],
1135	                row["founderId"],
1136	                upload_id,
1137	                "ingest",
1138	                _db_safe(audit_detail, cap=500),
1139	                now,
1140	            ),
1141	        )
1142	
1143	        conn.commit()
1144	        return IngestFromCodexResult(
1145	            upload_id=upload_id,
1146	            title=row["title"] or row["originalName"] or upload_id,
1147	            num_claims_extracted=len(claims),
1148	            num_conclusions_written=written,
1149	            num_contradictions_written=contradictions_written,
1150	            num_open_questions_written=open_questions_written,
1151	            num_research_suggestions_written=research_suggestions_written,
1152	            dry_run=False,
1153	            mode=mode,
1154	        )
1155	    except Exception as outer_exc:
1156	        conn.rollback()
1157	        # Keep the structured errorMessage the inner dispatch-failure
1158	        # handler wrote — overwriting it with the generic "Local
1159	        # ingest-from-codex failed: …" wrapper loses the
1160	        # `unsupported_mime:` / `extraction_failed:` prefix the Codex
1161	        # dashboard and tests rely on. Any OTHER exception reaches this
1162	        # branch without anyone having marked the row, so we do need to
1163	        # mark it here.
1164	        already_classified = type(outer_exc).__name__ in (
1165	            "ExtractionFailed",
1166	            "UnsupportedMimeType",
1167	        )
1168	        if not already_classified:
1169	            try:
1170	                err = _open_codex_connection(url)
1171	                ec = err.cursor()
1172	                ec.execute(
1173	                    '''UPDATE "Upload"
1174	                       SET status = %s, "errorMessage" = %s
1175	                       WHERE id = %s''',
1176	                    (
1177	                        "failed",
1178	                        _db_safe(
1179	                            f"Local ingest-from-codex failed: {sys.exc_info()[1]}",
1180	                            cap=400,
1181	                        ),
1182	                        upload_id,
1183	                    ),
1184	                )
1185	                err.commit()
1186	                err.close()
1187	            except Exception:
1188	                pass

1190	    finally:
1191	        conn.close()

1194	def list_queued_uploads(
1195	    *, codex_db_url: Optional[str] = None, limit: int = 25
1196	) -> list[dict]:

1198	    the user can pick which one to process."""

1208	                  OR status IS NULL
